name: True Blue Tart
recipeIngredient: 6 tablespoons Butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
For pastry: Combine flour, butter, shortening and 1 tablespoon sugar in a medium bowl. Mix well. Gradually add ice water, stirring with a fork until the mixture reaches the consistency of coarse meal. Form dough into a ball. Knead on a floured surface for several seconds. Shape into a ball and dust with flour. Wrap dough in waxed paper and refrigerate for 1 hour. Roll dough into a 9-inch circle. Press into bottom and sides of a 9-inch pie plate or tart pan. Trim excess dough 1 inch beyond the rim of the pan. Turn excess under to make a narrow rolled rim. Flute decoratively or press edge with a fork. Prick the bottom of the pastry and chill for 1 hour. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line pastry with parchment paper or foil and fill with dried beans or rice. Bake until delicately browned, about 10 to 15 minutes. Discard paper and beans; let tart shell cool.
For sauce: Mix 1/2 cup sugar with cornstarch in a bowl.
Combine 4 cups blueberries, sugar, lemon juice, currant jelly and lemon peel in a medium saucepan. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring frequently. Cook, stirring constantly until thickened, about 10 minutes. Let cool completely. Transfer to a bowl and chill.
For glaze: Combine apricot preserves and brandy in a processor or blender. Puree. Paint a thin layer over tart shell. Store remaining glaze in the refrigerator. Spread remaining blueberries in the shell. Carefully pour sauce over top. Chill until set. Serve with cream.
